articles.title: Zimbabwe's iconic stone birds were taken by colonialists. Finally, they're all back home
articles.description: For centuries the prized sculptures, central to national identity, have been kept outside Zimbabwe's borders.

articles.title: Mugabe's son pleads guilty to pointing a gun in South Africa
articles.description: Bellarmine Mugabe was arrested in February following the shooting of a 23-year-old man at his home in Johannesburg.

articles.title: Zimbabwe play challenges proposed constitutional changes
articles.description: Bodo is a one-man performance that draws on Ngano, a traditional Shona storytelling method, blending it with modern visual and audio elements to create an immersive experience. Through this fusion of tradition and contemporary theatre, Guzha seeks not only to entertain but to provoke reflection

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Iran reverses decision to open Hormuz citing ‘US piracy under guise of blockade’

more.title: Rihanna makes history as first woman in music to achieve 200 million RIAA single certifications
more.description: Rihanna isn’t just a mogul; she’s a mathematical marvel. more.title: Zimbabwe cuts fuel prices, slightly
more.description: The Zimbabwe Energy Regulatory Authority (ZERA) has announced a slight reduction in fuel prices, effective immediately, following recent easing in global oil markets after Iran reopened the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping route that had been disrupted amid conflict involving Israel and the United States. more.title: Zimbabwean President’s Business Ally Pays Legislators $10k Each Ahead of Controversial Consitutional Amendment Vote
more.description: HARARE – Controversial businessman Wicknell Chivayo has announced he will donate USD$3.6 million to all 360 members of parliament and senators, saying he received personal approval from President Emmerson Mnangagwa for the gesture. more.title: Zimbabweans in UK stages embassy protest over Mnangagwapower-grab constitutional amendment
more.description: LONDON, United Kingdom – Zimbabweans living in the United Kingdom will gather outside Zimbabwe House on The Strand in central London on Saturday – Independence Day – to protest the Constitution of Zimbabwe Amendment (No. 3) Bill, demanding that President Emmerson Mnangagwa’s government submit any constitutional changes to a national referendum.
